'''Darrell Goulding''' (born 3 March 1988) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer. He spent nine years of his career with [[Wigan Warriors]], with whom he won the [[Super League Grand Final]] in 2010 and 2013, and also played for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] and [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] in the [[Super League]].<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> He played as either a {{rlp|ce}} or on the {{rlp|wg}}.

==Early life==
Goulding was born in [[Wigan]], [[Greater Manchester]], England, he is a former St. Edmund Arrowsmith pupil. he played for the junior amateur club Wigan St. Patrick's in [[Ince-in-Makerfield|Ince]], Wigan. He was part of Wigan's scholarship scheme and played in Wigan's Academy U18s in 2004 and U21s in 2005.

==Club career==
===Wigan===
Goulding signed a two-year full-time contract with Wigan in July 2005, and was drafted into the first team [[squad]] for the first time in August 2005 and shortly after made his début against Widnes.

Goulding scored his first try for Wigan in the 48-22 loss to [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] on 9 June 2006.
[[File:Darrell Goulding.jpg|thumb|right|Goulding while playing for Wigan in 2007]]
On 6 April 2009, [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] centre [[Martin Gleeson (rugby league)|Martin Gleeson]] transferred to Wigan in a deal rumoured to involve Goulding, since he had only been played twice in Wigan's first ten Super League matches. However, this rumour was rejected when Goulding was released on loan to [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] on 14 April.

Goulding made his Reds' début in [[Perpignan]], France in the defeat by the [[Catalans Dragons]]. He made his home début a week later at the Willows, scoring two tries - including a 70-metre interception score against Hull. He added a third for the Salford club in his next Super League game - the away victory at Huddersfield.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/salford/7998640.stm|title=Goulding makes Salford loan move|publisher=news.bbc.co.uk|access-date=2009-04-17| date=14 April 2009}}</ref>

After returning to Wigan in 2010, Goulding had a breakthrough year, filling in for the injured [[Amos Roberts]] for the majority of games.{{cn|date=April 2021}} He finished the year with 20 appearances and 25 tries, joint second highest scorer in 2010.<ref>http://www.superleague.co.uk/statistics_player.php?hs1=2010&hp1=1806&hs2=2010&hp2=263&stat=tries&season=2010{{dead link|date=January 2018 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes }}</ref>

Goulding played in the [[2010 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite news|last=Wilson|first=Andy|title=Wigan turn St Helens into sinners with Super League Grand Final win|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2010/oct/02/wigan-st-helens-grand-final|access-date=21 November 2019|newspaper=guardian.co.uk|date=2 October 2010}}</ref>

His [[2011 Wigan Warriors season|2011 season]] started against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] at the season-opening [[Magic Weekend]] event.<ref>{{cite news|last=Scott|first=Ged|title=St Helens 16-16 Wigan|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/9393298.stm|access-date=7 May 2011|newspaper=BBC|date=12 February 2011}}</ref> After opening his try tally at [[Bradford Bulls]] in Round 2, he scored again at [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] in Round 4 and featured in the [[2011 World Club Challenge]] team that lost to [[St George Illawarra Dragons]].<ref>{{cite news|title=Bradford 10-44 Wigan|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/9400211.stm|access-date=7 May 2011|newspaper=BBC|date=20 February 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|last=Wilson|first=Andy|title=Wigan Warriors make brave attempt but Dragons are still worlds apart|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2011/feb/27/wigan-st-george-illawarra-world-club-challenge|access-date=7 May 2011|newspaper=The Guardian|date=27 February 2011}}</ref> Goulding made five further consecutive appearances, including a try in Round 8 against [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]], but was dropped after their loss in Round 9 to [[Catalans Dragons]], in which Goulding's error under the high ball had handed [[Frédéric Vaccari]] a try.<ref>{{cite news|title=Leeds Rhinos 22-22 Wigan Warriors|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/9439007.stm|access-date=7 May 2011|newspaper=BBC|date=1 April 2011}}</ref> He reappeared for Wigan's Round 13 victory over [[Crusaders Rugby League|Crusaders RL]].<ref>{{cite news|title=Crusaders 16-48 Wigan Warriors|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/9469383.stm|access-date=7 May 2011|newspaper=BBC|date=1 May 2011}}</ref>

He played in the [[2013 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over [[Hull F.C.]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url= https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/23794947|title= Hull FC 0-16 Wigan Warriors|date=24 August 2013|work= BBC Sport|access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url= https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2013/aug/24/hull-fc-wigan-warriors-challenge-cup-final|title= Wigan Warriors overcome Hull FC and elements to win Challenge Cup|date=24 August 2013|work= Guardian|access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url= http://www.skysports.com/rugbyleague/match_report/0,,11070_3660839,00.html|title= Wigan Warriors grind out victory over Hull|date=24 August 2013|work= Sky Sports|access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref>

It was Goulding who also scored the try just before half time in Wigan's 2013 Grand Final win over the [[Warrington Wolves]]. His try brought the score back to 16-6 after the Warriors went 16-2 down and Wigan came back in the second half to win 30-16 at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite web|title=Super League Grand Final |url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/super_league/grandfinal |publisher=Super League |access-date=14 July 2013 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130729050226/http://www.superleague.co.uk/super_league/grandfinal |archive-date=29 July 2013}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|last=Newsum|first=Matt|title=Super League Grand Final: Wigan Warriors beat Warrington|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/24384930|access-date=13 November 2019|newspaper=BBC Sport|date=5 October 2013}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|title=Super League Grand Final: Warrington v Wigan|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/0/rugby-league/24339698|archive-url=https://archive.today/20131006111732/http://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/0/rugby-league/24339698|url-status=dead|archive-date=6 October 2013|work=BBC Sport|access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/10358228/Super-League-Grand-Final-2013-Warrington-Wolves-16-Wigan-Warriors-30-match-report.html|title= Warrington Wolves 16 Wigan Warriors 30|date=5 October 2013|work=Daily Telegraph|access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2013/oct/05/warrington-wigan-super-league-grand-final|title= Wigan see off Warrington in X-rated Grand Final to complete double|date=5 October 2013|work=Guardian |access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref>

===Hull KR===
In July 2014, [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] announced that Goulding had signed a three-year contract with the club starting from the 2015 season.<ref>{{cite web|title=Darrell Goulding joins Hull KR from Wigan Warriors|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/28097665|website=BBC Sport|access-date=5 November 2014|date=1 July 2014}}</ref> Just 8 matches into his Hull KR career, however, Goulding was forced to retire from the sport due to a string of concussion injuries. His retirement was formally announced on Monday 1 June 2015.<ref>[https://www.bbc.com/sport/0/rugby-league/32964870 Darrell Goulding: Hull KR centre forced to retire aged 27]</ref>

==International career==
Goulding was selected for [[Lancashire]] U17s, England U15 and U16s while part of the Wigan scholarship system. He was also included in the England U17s squad to take on the touring [[Australian Institute of Sport]] in 2005. In September 2010 he was called into the senior England squad for the first time for the [[2010 Four Nations]] series in Australia and New Zealand, starting against the Kiwis in a 24-10 defeat in Wellington and also against the Kangaroos in a 34-14 defeat in Melbourne.
